subject: electricity
explanation: Student leaves time in minutes instead of converting to seconds when using formulas.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't recognize that SI units require seconds, or forgets conversion step.
grade8_impact: This error persists across all physics topics - kinematics, thermodynamics, etc.
grade12_impact: Will consistently lose marks on unit-dependent calculations.
root_cause: Weak understanding of SI unit system and dimensional analysis

subject: electricity
explanation: Student divides instead of multiplies or vice versa when applying formulas.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand what the formula represents physically, just memorizes symbols.
grade8_impact: Will make operation errors across all formula-based topics.
root_cause: Formula memorization without understanding the relationship being expressed

subject: electricity
explanation: Student makes multiple errors indicating fundamental confusion about the concept.
why_students_think_this: Lacks foundational understanding of both the concept and mathematical operations.
grade8_impact: Will struggle with all quantitative physics without intervention.
root_cause: Multiple foundational gaps - needs comprehensive support

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Multiplies instead of divides in V=W/Q
explanation: Student uses V = W × Q instead of V = W/Q.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand 'per charge' means division, or confuses with other formulas.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand any 'per unit' quantities - density, pressure, field strength.
root_cause: Doesn't understand what 'per' means mathematically

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Confuses units of voltage and work
explanation: Gets the right number but wrong units - doesn't know voltage is measured in Volts.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand what each quantity represents physically.
grade8_impact: Will lose marks on exams, can't check if answers make sense.
root_cause: Weak connection between physical quantities and their units

subject: electricity
explanation: Uses V = Q/W instead of V = W/Q.
why_students_think_this: Guesses at formula structure without understanding the relationship.
grade8_impact: Will invert other formulas randomly, leading to systematic errors.
root_cause: No systematic approach to understanding formulas

subject: electricity
explanation: Treats 0.5 as 5, or makes other decimal calculation errors.
why_students_think_this: Careless with decimal arithmetic or doesn't understand place value well.
grade8_impact: Persistent calculation errors across all topics.
root_cause: Weak arithmetic fundamentals with decimals

subject: electricity
explanation: Uses V = I/R or similar incorrect rearrangement.
why_students_think_this: Weak algebra skills or doesn't understand the relationship.
grade8_impact: Cannot solve for different variables in any formula.
root_cause: Weak algebraic manipulation skills

subject: electricity
explanation: Gets calculation right but uses Ω instead of V or vice versa.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't distinguish between the physical quantities.
grade8_impact: Consistent unit confusion across electrical concepts.
root_cause: Weak understanding of what each quantity represents

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Cannot rearrange formula - multiplies instead of divides
explanation: When solving for I, uses I = V × R instead of I = V/R.
why_students_think_this: Cannot perform algebraic rearrangement of formulas.
grade8_impact: Cannot solve physics problems requiring formula manipulation.
root_cause: Fundamental algebra weakness

subject: electricity
explanation: Uses I = R/V instead of I = V/R.
why_students_think_this: Guesses at rearrangement without systematic approach.
grade8_impact: Systematic errors in all formula-based problems.
root_cause: No understanding of algebraic rearrangement principles

subject: electricity
explanation: Knows the method but makes calculation error.
why_students_think_this: Carelessness or weak arithmetic fundamentals.
grade8_impact: Minor - can be addressed with more careful work.
root_cause: Carelessness or weak basic arithmetic

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Decimal calculation error in power problems
explanation: Treats 0.5 as 5 or makes other decimal errors in power calculations.
why_students_think_this: Careless with decimals.
grade8_impact: Consistent calculation errors.
root_cause: Weak decimal arithmetic

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Confuses power units with voltage units
explanation: Calculation correct but writes V instead of W for power.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't know power is measured in Watts.
grade8_impact: Unit confusion across power-related topics.
root_cause: Weak understanding of power as a physical quantity

subject: electricity
explanation: Uses P = I/V or other incorrect formula.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand power relationship, guesses at formula.
grade8_impact: Cannot calculate power in any context.
root_cause: No understanding of what power represents

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks current and charge are the same thing, doesn't understand current as rate of charge flow.
why_students_think_this: Sees charge flowing and thinks that's current. Doesn't grasp 'per unit time' concept.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand current density, drift velocity, AC vs DC.
grade12_impact: Cannot understand electromagnetic induction, Maxwell's equations.
root_cause: Doesn't understand rate concepts in general

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks current is opposition to charge flow rather than flow itself.
why_students_think_this: Fundamental confusion between basic electrical quantities.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand any circuit analysis.
root_cause: All basic quantities confused - needs ground-up rebuilding

subject: electricity
explanation: Confuses current with energy or power.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't distinguish between I, V, P, E.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand energy calculations in circuits.
root_cause: No clear mental model of different electrical quantities

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks V = W (ignores the 'per charge' part of the definition).
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand intensive vs extensive properties.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand electric field, capacitance, any field concepts.
grade12_impact: Cannot understand potential energy, field theory.
root_cause: Doesn't understand intensive properties (per unit quantities)

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks voltage is rate of charge flow.
why_students_think_this: Fundamental confusion between V and I.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand any electrical concepts.
root_cause: All basic quantities confused

subject: electricity
explanation: Cannot distinguish between V and R.
why_students_think_this: All electrical quantities are jumbled together.
grade8_impact: Cannot do any circuit analysis.
root_cause: Fundamental confusion requiring complete restart

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Thinks battery determines current at all points
explanation: Doesn't understand current is determined by total circuit resistance.
why_students_think_this: Oversimplified understanding - battery as sole determinant.
grade8_impact: Cannot analyze circuits with multiple components.
root_cause: Doesn't understand relationship between V, I, and R in circuits

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks current is same because resistance is same (when it isn't).
why_students_think_this: Confused logic about what determines current.
grade8_impact: Cannot reason about circuit behavior.
root_cause: Weak causal reasoning about electrical relationships

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Thinks current is consumed in circuit
explanation: Believes current gets used up by components, doesn't understand conservation.
why_students_think_this: Thinks of current like fuel being consumed.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand Kirchhoff's laws, any advanced circuits.
root_cause: Doesn't understand conservation of charge

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Thinks Ohm's law is always valid
explanation: Doesn't know Ohm's law has conditions (constant temperature, ohmic materials).
why_students_think_this: Thinks it's a universal law like F=ma.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand non-ohmic devices, semiconductors, diodes.
root_cause: Doesn't distinguish between universal laws and conditional relationships

subject: electricity
explanation: Creates impossible conditions like 'voltage must be greater than current'.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand V and I are different types of quantities.
grade8_impact: Fundamental confusion about electrical quantities.
root_cause: No understanding of what quantities represent

subject: electricity
explanation: Says resistance is constant, but doesn't explain why (temperature constant).
why_students_think_this: Surface-level understanding without deeper reasoning.
grade8_impact: Minor - can be addressed with follow-up questions.
root_cause: Needs deeper conceptual exploration

subject: electricity
explanation: Cannot extract slope meaning from graph.
why_students_think_this: Weak graphical analysis skills.
grade8_impact: Will struggle with all physics graphs, lab work, data analysis.
root_cause: Weak graph interpretation skills across subjects

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Brings in concepts not shown in graph
explanation: Mentions power, charge, or other concepts not represented in V-I graph.
why_students_think_this: Random guessing with physics vocabulary.
grade8_impact: Cannot focus on what a specific representation shows.
root_cause: Doesn't understand what each graph/diagram represents

subject: electricity
explanation: Completely lost - no understanding of any concepts.
why_students_think_this: Multiple foundational gaps, or not engaged with material.
grade8_impact: Cannot proceed without intensive intervention.
root_cause: Needs ground-up rebuilding of electrical concepts

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Thinks voltage scales with total work done
explanation: Doesn't understand V is intensive (work PER charge) - thinks V increases with total work.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand intensive vs extensive properties.
grade8_impact: Cannot understand electric field, capacitance, any field/potential concepts.
grade12_impact: Cannot understand field theory, gravitational potential, etc.
root_cause: Fundamental misunderstanding of 'per unit' quantities

subject: electricity
explanation: Completely ignores Q in V = W/Q.
why_students_think_this: Didn't even attempt the calculation correctly.
grade8_impact: Cannot do any voltage calculations correctly.

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Brings in factors not relevant to the question
explanation: Thinks resistance or distance is needed when only W and Q matter.
why_students_think_this: Doesn't understand what determines potential difference.
grade8_impact: Cannot isolate relevant variables in problems.

subject: electricity
explanation: Knows ammeter has low resistance, voltmeter has high resistance, but doesn't know WHY they're placed differently.
why_students_think_this: Memorized properties without understanding function.

subject: electricity
explanation: Doesn't understand placement is functionally necessary.
why_students_think_this: Sees it as a rule to memorize, not a logical necessity.

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks ammeter measures voltage, voltmeter measures current.
why_students_think_this: Names not connected to function.
grade8_impact: Lab safety risk - could damage equipment.
root_cause: Names memorized without functional understanding

explanation: Calculates using only one resistor, ignoring others in series.

explanation: Ignores all resistors except the first one.

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks ammeter goes in parallel (voltmeter's placement).

grade8_impact: Lab safety risk - will blow ammeter.

subject: electricity
explanation: Thinks series or parallel doesn't matter for instruments.
why_students_think_this: No understanding of how instruments work.

subject: electricity
explanation: Would create short circuit or other dangerous configuration.
why_students_think_this: No understanding of circuits whatsoever.

subject: electricity
misconception_name: Forgets to include time in energy calculation
explanation: Calculates cost using power alone, forgets time duration.

explanation: Uses 1000 W directly instead of converting to 1 kW.
